rationale: The citation is used in both the literature review and model development to support a core behavioral premise: consumers differ in whether they focus on gains or losses relative to a reference point. The authors then build this directly into their demand model via structural heterogeneity, assigning households probabilities of being gain-focused versus loss-focused. So the paper is not merely listing prior work; it uses this prior finding to justify a central component of its conceptual and empirical setup.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Erdem, Mayhew, and Sun (2001) as prior evidence for heterogeneity in gain-versus-loss reference-price responses. It uses that evidence as a conceptual basis for its own gain-focused versus loss-focused mixture structure in modeling quantity-discount effects.
